Test Description: Default reset-value verification and masked write/read verification across 49 GPIO-related registers using read/write masks and expected default values.
Remarks:
- VRRW registers are skipped as per skip_array comment.
- Note from source: when reading default values the DIN value becomes 1 automatically if no force is applied; forcing DIN to zero makes level select high, which can cause default read mismatch if not handled.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point(s):
- int test_case()

Runtime execution trace (strict order):
1) CALL chk_rst_val()
   1.1) FOR i = 0..(CNT-1) [CNT=49]
        - addr = addr_array[i]  // addr_array contains: MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8..GPIO_39, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR2_INTR_EN1, MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR2_INTR_STS1, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1..4, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DOUT_GROUP1..4, MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_DIN_GROUP1..4
        - IF (skip_rst_array[i] == 1) CONTINUE
        - IF (read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) CONTINUE
        - READ: data_rd = read_reg(addr)
        - COMPUTE: data = (data_rd & 0xFFFFFFFE)
        - IF (data == default_value_array[i]) { PASS path } ELSE { def_fail_cnt++; printf failure }

2) CALL chk_rd_wr()
   2.1) DEFINE: unsigned int chk_val[6] = {0xFFFFFFFF, 0xAAAAAAAA, 0x55555555, 0xF5F5F5F5, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xFFFF0000}
   2.2) FOR j = 0..5
        - data_wr = chk_val[j]
        - WRITE PHASE: FOR i = 0..(CNT-1)
            * addr = addr_array[i]
            * IF (skip_array[i] == 1) CONTINUE
            * IF (write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) CONTINUE
            * WRITE: write_reg(addr, (data_wr & write_mask_array[i]))
        - READ/VERIFY PHASE: FOR i = 0..(CNT-1)
            * addr = addr_array[i]
            * IF (skip_array[i] == 1) CONTINUE
            * IF (write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) CONTINUE
            * IF (read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000) CONTINUE
            * READ: data_rd = (read_reg(addr) & read_mask_array[i])
            * COMPUTE: wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xFFFFFFFF)
            * COMPUTE: exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i]))
            * IF (data_rd == exp_val) { PASS path } ELSE { wr_fail_cnt++; printf mismatch }

3) TERMINATE: IF (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) finish(1) ELSE finish(0)

Timing:
- No active delay/wait in executed paths (soft_reset_chk is compiled out with #ifdef 0).

Test Description: Negative-edge interrupt enable and verification for GPIO[8..39] with per-pin configuration, group enable, generated falling edges, ISR service, and clear verification.
Remarks:
- Arm wait BEFORE generating the edge to avoid race.
- Bounded wait with timeout=5000 to prevent infinite loop.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point(s):
- int test_case()
- ISR: void Default_IRQHandler()

Runtime execution trace (strict order):
A) test_case()
1) INIT: test_err = 0
2) IFDEF GPIO0: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(87); IFDEF GPIO1: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(88)
3) Enable sysreg interrupt:
   - IFDEF GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 <= LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR
   - IFDEF GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 <= LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR
4) DRIVE known state high: WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= 0xFFFFFFFF
5) CONFIG per-pin (i = 0..31):
   - addr1 = (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4)
   - WRITE addr1 <= ((1<<20) | (1<<18) | (1<<16))  // doe=1, neie=1, iclr=1
   - CALL wait_on(10)
6) PER-PIN sequence (i = 0..31):
   - wr_val = (1u << i)
   - WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1 <= wr_val  // clear any latched raw
   - WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1   <= wr_val  // enable only this bit
   - CALL wait_on(10)
   - int_pend = 1                                  // arm BEFORE edge
   - WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= 0xFFFFFFFF                // ensure high
   - CALL wait_on(30)
   - WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= ~wr_val                   // create falling edge on bit i
   - timeout = 5000
   - WHILE (int_pend && timeout--) { wait_on(10) }
   - IF (timeout == 0) { printf timeout; test_err++ }
7) CALL finish(test_err)

B) Default_IRQHandler()
1) local_wr = (1u << i)
2) int_pend = 0
3) WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= 0xFFFFFFFF                  // return pads high
4) raddr = (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4)
5) READ rdata = read_reg(raddr)
6) VALIDATE DIN for negedge: IF ((rdata & 0x1) != 0) { test_err++ }
7) CHECK raw bit: IF ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0) THEN
   7.1) READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
        - IF ((rdata_grp & local_wr) == 0) { test_err++ }
   7.2) WRITE (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) <= ((1<<20) | (1<<16))  // doe=1, iclr=1
   7.3) WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_INTR_RAW_STCLR1 <= local_wr
   7.4) READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
        - IF (rdata_grp != 0x0) { test_err++ }
   7.5) IFDEF GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 <= LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR; CALL GIC_ClearIRQ(87)
        IFDEF GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 <= LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR; CALL GIC_ClearIRQ(88)
   ELSE { test_err++ }
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Timeout loop: PASS if (int_pend cleared) before timeout; FAIL if timeout reaches 0.
- DIN check after negedge: PASS if ((rdata & 0x1) == 0); FAIL otherwise.
- Raw bit present: PASS if ((rdata & 0x2) != 0x0); FAIL otherwise.
- Group status set: PASS if ((read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & (1u<<i)) != 0); FAIL otherwise.
- Group clear verification: after clear ops, PASS if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) == 0x0); FAIL otherwise.
- Overall: finish(test_err) with test_err == 0 => PASS; else FAIL.

Test Description: Positive-edge interrupt enable and verification for GPIO[8..39] with per-pin configuration, group enable, generated rising edges, ISR service, and clear verification.
Remarks:
- Arm BEFORE edge; bounded wait with timeout=2000 to avoid hangs.
- Group interrupt masked during service inside ISR.
Test Steps / Procedure:
Entry point(s):
- void test_case()
- ISR: void Default_IRQHandler()

Runtime execution trace (strict order):
A) test_case()
1) IFDEF GPIO0: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(87); IFDEF GPIO1: CALL GIC_EnableIRQ(88)
2) Enable sysreg interrupt:
   - IFDEF GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 <= LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO0_INTR
   - IFDEF GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1 <= LSS_SYSREG_INTR_EN1_GPIO1_INTR
3) CONFIG posedge per pin (i = 0..31):
   - WRITE (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + i*4) <= 0x00020000   // posedge enable (bit17=1)
4) CALL wait_on(10)
5) Set input mode groups:
   - WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP1 <= 0x000000FF
   - WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP2 <= 0x000000FF
   - WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP3 <= 0x000000FF
   - WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GPIO_IO_CTRL_GROUP4 <= 0x000000FF
6) CALL wait_on(10)
7) Enable group interrupts: WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 <= 0xFFFFFFFF
8) PER-PIN trigger loop (i = 0..31):
   - WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= 0x00000000       // drive low
   - CALL wait_on(10)
   - int_pend = 1                         // arm BEFORE edge
   - WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= 0xFFFFFFFF       // rising edge
   - timeout = 2000
   - WHILE ((int_pend == 1) && (--timeout > 0)) { wait_on(10) }
   - IF (timeout == 0) { printf error; test_err++; break }
   - WRITE 0xA0243ffc <= 0x00000000
   - CALL wait_on(10)
9) CALL finish(test_err)

B) Default_IRQHandler()
1) wr_val = (1 << i)
2) int_pend = 0
3) READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
4) WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 <= 0x00000000   // mask during service
5) IF ((rdata_grp & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0) { // group IRQ present } ELSE { printf error; test_err++ }
6) CLEAR per-pin raw (j = 0..31): WRITE (MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_GPIO_8 + j*4) <= 0x00010000
7) CALL wait_on(2)
8) READ rdata_grp = read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1)
   - IF (rdata_grp == 0x0) { // cleared } ELSE { printf error; test_err++ }
9) Sysreg clear and verify:
   - IFDEF GPIO0: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 <= LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR; READ rdata = read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); IF ((rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO0_INTR) != 0) { printf not cleared; test_err++ }
   - IFDEF GPIO1: WRITE MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1 <= LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR; READ rdata = read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1); IF ((rdata & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIO1_INTR) != 0) { printf not cleared; test_err++ }
10) WRITE MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_EN1 <= 0xFFFFFFFF  // re-enable
11) IFDEF GPIO0: CALL GIC_ClearIRQ(87); IFDEF GPIO1: CALL GIC_ClearIRQ(88)
Validation / Acceptance Criteria:
- Timeout loop: PASS if (int_pend cleared) before timeout; FAIL if timeout reaches 0.
- Group interrupt present: PASS if ((read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) & 0xFFFFFFFF) != 0); FAIL otherwise.
- Group clear verification: after per-pin clear and wait_on(2), PASS if (read_reg(MIZAR_GPIO_GP0_INTR1_INTR_STS1) == 0x0); FAIL otherwise.
- Sysreg clear verification: after RAW_STCR1 write, PASS if ((read_reg(MIZAR_LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1) & LSS_SYSREG_RAW_STCR1_GPIOx_INTR) == 0); FAIL otherwise.
- Overall: finish(test_err) with test_err == 0 => PASS; else FAIL.
